title: Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02)
summary: Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Loughborough University focusing on the technical, legal, and socio-cultural complexities involved in communicating multi-hazard early warning systems to jurisdictional agencies and emergency response partners. Hosted within the School of Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering, this doctoral research project forms part of the prestigious Informatics for Multi-hazard Risk and Resilience (i-Risk) NERC Doctoral Focal Awards in Environmental Sciences. The project is supervised by Prof Dilanthi Amaratunga and Dr John Hillier and offers an outstanding opportunity for researchers interested in disaster risk reduction, climate resilience, governance systems, informatics, civil engineering, and emergency management. With global climate risks intensifying and disaster events becoming increasingly complex, the need for effective Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems (MHEWS) has become a major international priority. This PhD project aims to investigate one of the most overlooked areas within disaster governance: the interface between hazard detection systems and operational emergency response agencies. About the PhD Project The research project was developed in response to the devastating coastal floods that affected South Asia in late 2025, highlighting urgent global weaknesses in disaster preparedness and emergency communication systems. Although governance structures are widely recognized as essential to sustainable disaster management systems, the transition point between upstream hazard monitoring systems and downstream emergency response operations remains poorly understood. This “interface” is often characterized by: Jurisdictional complexity Institutional fragmentation Communication barriers Legal coordination challenges Technical interoperability issues Socio-cultural response dynamics The PhD research seeks to examine these governance arrangements in detail and identify ways to strengthen coordination between: Regional information providers Hazard detection systems National agencies Sub-national emergency operations centers Disaster response stakeholders Ultimately, the project aims to contribute toward long-term disaster resilience and improved multi-hazard preparedness frameworks globally. Research Areas and Academic Focus This interdisciplinary PhD combines multiple fields including: Civil Engineering Climate Science Politics and Government Disaster Risk Reduction Environmental Informatics Governance and Public Policy Emergency Management Resilience Studies The project is particularly suitable for candidates interested in understanding how technology, governance, and human systems interact during disaster response and resilience planning. Funding Information This is a fully funded UKRI studentship covering 3.5 years of doctoral study. Funding includes: A tax-free stipend of £21,805 per year Tuition fees at the UK rate Potential full international tuition fee waiver for outstanding international applicants The programme notes that, due to UKRI funding regulations, no more than 30% of funded studentships can be awarded to international students. Funding Duration Full-time: 3.5 years Part-time: 7 years Start Date October 2026 Bench Fees No bench fees required Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Hold, or expect to obtain shortly, a strong undergraduate or Master’s degree Have at least a UK 2:1 honours degree or equivalent international qualification Graduate from a recognized high-ranking university Demonstrate strong academic and research potential Relevant academic backgrounds may include: Civil Engineering Climate Science Environmental Science Disaster Risk Management Geography Political Science Public Policy Data Science Informatics Governance Studies Related interdisciplinary fields English Language Requirements International applicants must meet the university’s English language requirements. Applicants are expected to achieve: IELTS score of 6.5 overall Minimum 6.0 in each competency Equivalent TOEFL or recognized English proficiency qualifications may also be accepted. What Applicants Must Submit During the online application process, candidates must upload: Personal Statement A two-page personal statement divided into two sections: Section One One page discussing: Research interests in informatics and disaster risk reduction Interest in the i-Risk Doctoral Focal Award Rationale for selecting the project Section Two Responses to four reflective questions addressing: Problem-solving and innovation Data analytics experience Goal-setting and motivation Handling setbacks and troubleshooting Additional Documents Applicants must also provide: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic transcripts Degree certificates IELTS or TOEFL certificate (if applicable) Application Assessment Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Motivation and career aspirations Intellectual excellence and research potential Suitability for the specific project Alignment with the i-Risk programme Interview Process Shortlisted candidates are expected to attend remote interviews via Microsoft Teams during the week commencing 29 June 2026. Applicants are also encouraged to contact supervisors before applying to discuss project-specific questions. How to Apply Applications must be submitted online through Loughborough University. Applicants should: Select “Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering” under programme name Quote the reference: IRISK-26-LU-02 Important Contacts General enquiries: irisk@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk School contact: abce.pgr@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk Why Study at Loughborough University? Loughborough University is internationally recognized for excellence in teaching, research, and postgraduate education. The university has received recognition through the FindAMasters and FindAPhD Postgrad Awards and is known for: High-quality research supervision Strong doctoral training environments International academic reputation Supportive postgraduate community Interdisciplinary research opportunities Doctoral researchers at Loughborough gain opportunities to contribute new knowledge to globally significant challenges while developing advanced academic, analytical, and professional skills. Application Deadline 📅 Closing Date: 9 June 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early due to the competitive nature of UKRI-funded doctoral positions. Final Thoughts This fully funded PhD at Loughborough University offers a rare opportunity to conduct impactful interdisciplinary research at the intersection of disaster governance, climate resilience, informatics, and emergency response systems. For students passionate about disaster risk reduction, policy coordination, climate resilience, and sustainable governance systems, this project provides not only financial support but also access to a world-class research environment tackling some of the most pressing global challenges of our time. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity 2026: Multi-Million-Dollar Investment Support for Agribusinesses in Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Agribusinesses across Sub-Saharan Africa now have the opportunity to access large-scale financing and growth support through the 2026 AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity, an investment initiative designed to strengthen Africa’s agricultural sector and accelerate sustainable food systems development. Led by AgDevCo , this financing opportunity supports commercially viable agribusinesses that are ready to scale operations, expand market reach, improve agricultural productivity, and create measurable development impact across African economies. Unlike conventional grants or short-term startup funding programmes, AgDevCo provides long-term investment solutions tailored to growth-stage agribusinesses operating across the agricultural value chain. The initiative focuses on businesses capable of driving transformation in African food systems while creating employment opportunities, supporting smallholder farmers, and contributing to environmentally sustainable agricultural development. What Is the AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity? The AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity is a major investment platform that provides strategic financing and technical support to agribusinesses operating in Sub-Saharan Africa. The programme is specifically designed for businesses with proven operational models, strong management structures, and scalable commercial potential. AgDevCo invests in enterprises that can generate both financial returns and long-term development impact. The organization prioritizes investments capable of strengthening food systems, improving agricultural productivity, increasing market access, and supporting inclusive economic growth across Africa. Rather than offering one-size-fits-all funding, AgDevCo structures investments based on the needs, stage, and growth potential of each business. Types of Financial Support Available Selected agribusinesses may benefit from several forms of financing and growth support, including: Long-Term Debt Financing AgDevCo provides patient capital through long-term debt structures designed to support business expansion, infrastructure development, operational scaling, and production growth. Equity Investment Businesses with high growth potential may receive equity investment to support long-term strategic expansion and market development. Working Capital Support Agribusinesses often face liquidity and seasonal cash-flow challenges. AgDevCo offers working capital financing to help businesses maintain operational stability and growth momentum. Technical Assistance Beyond financing, selected businesses may also receive technical assistance aimed at strengthening operations, improving governance, enhancing ESG compliance, and supporting sustainable scaling. This blended support model helps agribusinesses not only secure capital but also build the institutional and operational capacity required for long-term success. Priority Investment Areas AgDevCo welcomes applications from agribusinesses operating across multiple segments of the agricultural value chain. Priority sectors include: Primary Production and Farming This includes crop farming, livestock production, aquaculture, and other forms of agricultural primary production capable of improving food security and economic inclusion. Agro-Processing and Value Addition Businesses involved in transforming raw agricultural products into higher-value goods are strongly encouraged to apply. Agricultural Inputs and Distribution Companies providing seeds, fertilizers, mechanization, irrigation systems, and agricultural technologies are eligible for consideration. Cold Chain and Logistics AgDevCo supports infrastructure and logistics solutions that improve storage, transportation, and post-harvest management across food systems. Market Systems and Aggregation Enterprises improving market access, aggregation systems, supply chain coordination, and trade efficiency may qualify for support. Climate-Smart Agriculture The programme prioritizes sustainable agricultural solutions capable of improving resilience, reducing environmental impact, and supporting climate adaptation. Food Systems Infrastructure Investments may also support infrastructure projects that strengthen food systems and agricultural market ecosystems. Funding Size and Investment Range One of the most significant aspects of this opportunity is the scale of financing available. AgDevCo does not impose a fixed funding amount. Instead, investments are structured according to the needs and growth potential of each business. Historically, AgDevCo investments have ranged from approximately US$1 million to over US$15 million. This makes the programme particularly suitable for established agribusinesses seeking substantial growth capital rather than early-stage startup grants. Who Can Apply? The opportunity is open to agribusinesses operating in Sub-Saharan Africa. Eligible applicants may include: Agricultural producers Agro-processing companies Agritech businesses Food system infrastructure companies Agricultural logistics providers Market aggregation enterprises Climate-smart agriculture ventures Agricultural supply chain businesses AgDevCo accepts applications from businesses operating in low- and middle-income countries across Sub-Saharan Africa. The organization may also consider investments in upper-middle-income countries such as South Africa where: There is strong development impact potential The business demonstrates regional expansion ambitions There is intent to invest in lower-income African markets Selection Criteria AgDevCo evaluates each application based on merit and alignment with its investment standards. Key selection criteria include: Proven Business Model Applicants should demonstrate a commercially viable and operational business model supported by a strong business plan and financial track record. Strong Revenue Performance Businesses should show evidence of consistent revenue generation and operational sustainability. Growth and Scaling Potential The programme prioritizes enterprises with clear opportunities for long-term expansion and market impact. Experienced Management Team A strong leadership and operational team is considered essential for successful investment partnerships. Development Impact AgDevCo seeks businesses capable of creating measurable positive impact, particularly for: Smallholder farmers Rural communities Job creation Food systems resilience Economic inclusion ESG and Regulatory Compliance Applicants must comply with applicable local and international regulations and demonstrate commitment to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) best practices. Commercial Sustainability Businesses should demonstrate the ability to achieve sustainable long-term commercial performance. Responsible Investment and Sustainability Responsible investment principles are central to AgDevCo’s approach. Potential investees are expected to: Comply with local and international laws Align with AgDevCo’s Responsible Business Principles Work toward international ESG standards Implement structured sustainability and governance improvement plans AgDevCo ultimately measures investment success not only through financial returns but also through long-term transformational impact on communities, economies, and the environment. Excluded Sectors AgDevCo supports projects across most agricultural value chains; however, businesses involved in: Tobacco Potable ethanol are not eligible for investment consideration. Final Thoughts The 2026 AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity represents a major opportunity for growth-stage agribusinesses seeking substantial capital and strategic support to scale operations across Africa. With investment sizes reaching multi-million-dollar levels and support extending beyond financing into technical assistance and ESG strengthening, AgDevCo offers a unique platform for businesses capable of driving sustainable transformation within African food systems. For agribusiness leaders committed to long-term impact, commercial sustainability, and inclusive agricultural development, this opportunity could serve as a powerful catalyst for expansion and regional growth. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity 2026: Multi-Million-Dollar Investment Support for Agribusinesses in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026: Win Funding, Meet Investors, and Scale Your Impact Across Africa
summary: Young African innovators now have an exciting opportunity to showcase transformative ideas, access investors, and compete for funding through the Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026, set to take place in Johannesburg, South Africa, on 8–9 October 2026. Organized by ICOYACA South Africa in partnership with Daraja Impact Hub Ltd and Africa Youth Forum International AYFI , this continental innovation challenge is designed to empower the next generation of African changemakers who are building scalable, technology-driven, and socially impactful solutions. The challenge creates a platform where ambitious young founders, innovators, entrepreneurs, and technology leaders can pitch solutions to real-world African challenges while gaining access to mentorship, funding opportunities, market-entry support, and investor networks. With Africa rapidly emerging as a global hub for youth-led innovation and entrepreneurship, initiatives like this challenge are helping bridge the gap between groundbreaking ideas and the resources needed to scale them into sustainable ventures. Why the Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge Matters Across Africa, many promising innovations fail to grow due to limited access to capital, mentorship, strategic partnerships, and visibility. While young innovators continue to develop solutions for pressing economic, environmental, and social challenges, many struggle to connect with the ecosystems capable of helping them scale. The Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge was created specifically to address this issue. Rather than functioning as just another competition, the initiative is structured as an innovation pipeline that connects young African innovators with practical growth opportunities. Participants are not only evaluated on their ideas but are also supported through accelerator-style mentorship, ecosystem access, and investor engagement opportunities. The challenge aims to amplify youth-led innovation while promoting solutions aligned with Africa’s future development priorities, including sustainability, digital transformation, trade, economic inclusion, and social impact. What Participants Will Gain Selected participants will benefit from a wide range of opportunities designed to support both early-stage and growth-stage innovations. Key benefits include: A structured accelerator and mentorship programme Direct investor and corporate engagement opportunities Continental visibility and networking opportunities Access to innovation ecosystems and strategic partners Live pitching opportunities during the Africa Youth Future Forward Summit in Johannesburg Practical market-entry and scaling support Exposure to industry leaders, ecosystem builders, and policymakers A chance to win the R20,000 Grand Prize funding The challenge is particularly valuable for innovators seeking not only financial support but also long-term ecosystem integration and strategic guidance. Focus Areas and Innovation Tracks Applicants are encouraged to submit innovative solutions that address major African development challenges through technology, entrepreneurship, sustainability, and social innovation. The challenge includes five major innovation tracks: 1. AI, Data and Digital Transformation This track focuses on digital innovation, artificial intelligence, big data, automation, and emerging technologies capable of transforming industries and public services across Africa. Innovators developing AI-driven platforms, digital tools, smart systems, cybersecurity solutions, educational technology, or data-driven services are encouraged to apply. 2. Climate, Energy and Green Economy Africa faces urgent environmental and climate-related challenges, creating demand for innovative green solutions. This track welcomes innovations in renewable energy, climate adaptation, waste management, sustainable agriculture, clean technology, circular economy models, and environmental sustainability. 3. Entrepreneurship and Economic Inclusion Economic empowerment remains central to Africa’s growth agenda. Applicants under this track may include solutions promoting financial inclusion, SME development, youth employment, digital finance, community entrepreneurship, or inclusive economic systems. 4. Youth Wellness and Social Impact This category focuses on innovations designed to improve social wellbeing and quality of life for African communities. Projects related to healthcare, mental health, education, youth empowerment, gender inclusion, social services, and community development are strongly encouraged. 5. Smart Trade and AfCFTA Enablement With the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) reshaping trade opportunities across the continent, this track focuses on solutions that facilitate cross-border trade, logistics, market integration, trade technology, and economic connectivity. Innovators building digital trade platforms, logistics systems, fintech trade tools, or market access solutions may find this category especially relevant. Event Location and Timeline The Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge will culminate during the Africa Youth Future Forward Summit in Johannesburg, South Africa, on 8–9 October 2026. The summit will bring together innovators, investors, ecosystem leaders, corporate stakeholders, and development actors from across the continent. Participants selected for live pitching will have the opportunity to showcase their innovations before a diverse audience capable of supporting scaling and implementation. Application Requirements Applicants are expected to prepare the following materials before submission: A PDF pitch deck A 3-minute video pitch Core implementation milestones for the next 12 months The application process is divided into five major sections: Applicant Information Team Information Innovation Details Implementation and Support Needs Declaration and Final Submission Applicants should ensure their submissions clearly explain: The problem being addressed The proposed solution The technology or innovation model used Scalability potential Social or economic impact Sustainability and implementation strategy Who Should Apply? The challenge is ideal for: Young entrepreneurs Startup founders Student innovators Tech innovators Social entrepreneurs Sustainability innovators Community change-makers Youth-led startups and teams Applicants with scalable and impact-driven ideas capable of addressing Africa’s pressing challenges are strongly encouraged to participate. Deadline At the time of publication, no official application deadline has been publicly announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications as early as possible ahead of the October 2026 summit. Final Thoughts The Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026 represents more than a competition. It is a strategic platform for empowering Africa’s next generation of innovators with the resources, exposure, and partnerships necessary to scale meaningful solutions. For young African founders working on transformative ideas, this challenge offers a rare opportunity to access funding, mentorship, investor networks, and continental visibility while contributing to Africa’s future development. As Africa continues to position itself as a global innovation hub, platforms like this play a critical role in unlocking the potential of youth-driven entrepreneurship and technology-led impact. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026: Win Funding, Meet Investors, and Scale Your Impact Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Evidence Action Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Development and Public Health Professionals
summary: Evidence Action has officially opened applications for the highly strategic and impactful position of Deputy Country Director in Nigeria for 2026. This senior leadership opportunity is designed for experienced professionals in international development, public health, program management, and government stakeholder engagement who are passionate about driving large-scale social impact across Africa. The position is based in Abuja, Nigeria, and offers candidates the opportunity to work with one of the world’s leading evidence-driven global health organizations. The selected candidate will help lead and scale transformative health and development interventions that have already impacted hundreds of millions of people globally. Professionals with strong leadership experience in multi-state program implementation, donor-funded initiatives, public health systems, and operational management are strongly encouraged to apply. About Evidence Action Evidence Action is a globally recognized nonprofit organization focused on delivering data-driven interventions that improve and save lives at massive scale. The organization specializes in identifying neglected global health issues and implementing solutions backed by rigorous academic evidence and operational efficiency. The organization works closely with governments, partners, and local communities to ensure sustainable impact across multiple countries. Currently operating in 11 countries with more than 900 staff members worldwide, Evidence Action has reached over 500 million people through innovative and evidence-based programs. Evidence Action’s Global Impact Evidence Action has built a strong reputation through several globally recognized interventions and programs. Deworm the World Program One of the organization’s flagship initiatives, Deworm the World, has delivered more than: 2 billion deworming treatments globally The program has significantly: Reduced worm prevalence Improved child health outcomes Increased school participation Generated over $23 billion in estimated lifetime productivity gains Safe Water Now Initiative Through its Safe Water Now initiative, Evidence Action has: Saved the lives of more than 15,000 children The program focuses on improving access to safe and clean drinking water in vulnerable communities. Accelerator Program Evidence Action’s Accelerator identifies and tests: Low-cost interventions High-impact health innovations Scalable public health solutions The goal is to explore untapped opportunities capable of dramatically improving health outcomes in underserved populations. Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026 Position Overview Job Title Deputy Country Director (Nigeria) Organization Evidence Action Job Type Full-Time Work Arrangement On-Site Location Abuja, Federal Capital Territory, Nigeria Role Summary The Deputy Country Director will serve as a senior member of Evidence Action’s Nigeria leadership team and will support the Country Director in overseeing the organization’s multi-state portfolio across Nigeria. The successful candidate will: Lead strategic implementation efforts Strengthen operational systems Support program execution Coordinate stakeholder relationships Enhance organizational performance Manage teams and partnerships Drive sustainable impact across Evidence Action programs The Deputy Country Director will also deputize for the Country Director during periods of absence and transition. This role requires a highly strategic, people-centered, and operationally strong professional capable of translating organizational goals into measurable results. Reporting Structure The Deputy Country Director will directly supervise or coordinate with: Zonal Managers Policy and Government Relations Senior Associate Nutrition Program Manager Nutrition Associates Associate Manager MLE-D (dotted line) Core Responsibilities The role combines strategic leadership, operational oversight, stakeholder engagement, financial management, and team development. 1. Strategy and Operations The Deputy Country Director will help shape and execute Evidence Action’s strategy in Nigeria. Key Duties Include: Supporting development of country strategies and annual plans Translating strategic goals into operational activities Managing implementation timelines and deliverables Resolving operational and programmatic risks Ensuring data-driven decision-making Supporting organizational compliance requirements Leading country operations in the absence of the Country Director The role requires strong systems thinking and strategic execution capabilities. 2. Program Performance Management The selected candidate will oversee implementation quality across multiple active states in Nigeria. Responsibilities Include: Monitoring service delivery standards Reviewing monitoring and evaluation data Identifying performance gaps Implementing corrective actions Coordinating with national and sub-national government stakeholders Supporting evidence-based programming Conducting field visits for implementation oversight Supporting pilot interventions and new program integration The candidate will ensure all interventions align with Evidence Action’s evidence-based operational model. 3. Team Leadership and Organizational Culture Evidence Action is seeking a leader capable of building high-performing and mission-driven teams. Team Leadership Duties: Mentoring and supervising country team members Promoting accountability and empowerment Supporting recruitment and onboarding processes Encouraging knowledge sharing and continuous learning Embedding organizational values into team culture Developing workforce capacity and performance The ideal candidate should possess excellent interpersonal and people management skills. 4. Resource and Financial Management The Deputy Country Director will support financial planning and donor accountability efforts. Key Financial Responsibilities: Supporting annual budgeting processes Conducting budget-versus-actual analyses Monitoring financial controls and compliance Supporting donor reporting requirements Encouraging cost-conscious program management Assisting with funding proposals and due diligence documentation Experience managing donor-funded programs is considered highly valuable for this role. 5. Government and Stakeholder Engagement Government collaboration is central to Evidence Action’s operational approach. The Successful Candidate Will: Represent Evidence Action at government forums Engage with ministries of health and stakeholders Maintain strategic partnerships Coordinate with civil society organizations Support advocacy and partnership strategies Identify potential program implementation partners Strong government relations experience is essential. 6. New Program Development The Deputy Country Director will also contribute to innovation and expansion initiatives. Responsibilities Include: Supporting pilot interventions Conducting landscape analyses Exploring high-potential interventions Assisting in proposal development Engaging prospective donors Supporting program scale-up efforts This component of the role is particularly suited for professionals passionate about innovation in public health and development. Key Competencies Required Evidence Action is seeking candidates with strong technical, strategic, and leadership competencies. Core Competencies Include: Strategic thinking Operational execution Team leadership Program performance management Budget and financial oversight Stakeholder engagement Donor relations Problem-solving Risk management Cross-cultural communication Collaboration and teamwork Minimum Qualifications and Experience Applicants must meet several important academic and professional requirements. Educational Requirements Candidates must possess: An advanced degree in: Public Health Health Management Social Sciences Development Studies Or related fields Professional Experience Requirements Applicants should have: At least 7 years of progressively responsible program management experience Minimum of 3 years in a senior leadership role Experience managing multi-site programs in Nigeria or Sub-Saharan Africa Strong team leadership experience Experience using data for program decisions Experience managing donor-funded projects Preferred Experience Candidates with the following experience will have an added advantage: Public health programming Nutrition programs WASH initiatives Neglected tropical disease programming Malaria interventions USAID-funded programs Gates Foundation projects GiveWell-funded initiatives FCDO-supported projects Language Requirements Applicants must: Be fluent in English Additional advantage for: Hausa Yoruba Other Nigerian languages Employee Benefits Evidence Action offers a competitive benefits package to support employee wellbeing and professional development. Benefits Include: Private Health Insurance Paid Time Off Training and Development Opportunities The organization also promotes flexibility, teamwork, innovation, and purpose-driven work. Why This Opportunity Is Important The Deputy Country Director role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to transformative health and development initiatives across Nigeria. Professionals selected for this role will: Influence large-scale public health systems Work directly with governments Lead high-impact programs Drive evidence-based interventions Improve lives across vulnerable communities This is an ideal opportunity for experienced development professionals seeking leadership roles with measurable social impact. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through Evidence Action’s official recruitment platform. Applicants are encouraged to prepare: An updated CV or Resume Relevant professional experience details Leadership and program management achievements Evidence of donor-funded project experience Apply HERE Equal Opportunity Statement Evidence Action is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. The organization does not discriminate based on: Race Gender Religion Disability Sexual orientation National origin Veteran status Age Or other legally protected characteristics For more information , visit the official website Discover more job opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Evidence Action Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Development and Public Health Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ASU and Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Announce Remote Africa-Based Coordinator Position
summary: The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program in partnership with Arizona State University (ASU) has officially opened applications for an exciting full-time remote Coordinator position designed for African professionals who are alumni of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program. This opportunity is specifically aimed at supporting Mastercard Foundation Scholars enrolled in ASU’s online graduate programs at Kepler College in Kigali, Rwanda. The selected coordinator will play a major role in academic support, virtual engagement, leadership development, and community-building for scholars from Sub-Saharan Africa. Professionals passionate about education, youth development, online learning, student engagement, and social impact are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline of May 25, 2026, at 12:00 PM GMT. About the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at ASU The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Arizona State University is committed to empowering young African leaders through accessible and innovative higher education opportunities. Through its collaboration with Kepler College in Kigali, Rwanda, ASU delivers online graduate education that supports leadership development, entrepreneurship, service, and community transformation. The newly announced Coordinator role is designed to strengthen scholar engagement and improve the online student experience for Mastercard Foundation Scholars pursuing graduate studies remotely. The position offers candidates the opportunity to contribute directly to educational transformation across Africa while working within a global and mission-driven team. Position Overview Job Title: Coordinator – Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at ASU (Africa-Based) Organization Arizona State University (ASU) in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Employment Type Full-Time Work Location Remote (Applicants must be based in Africa) Salary Range 36,000,000 RWF to 43,000,000 RWF Main Purpose of the Role Under the administrative direction of the Program Manager, the Coordinator will support Mastercard Foundation Scholars enrolled in ASU’s online graduate programs. The role focuses heavily on: Virtual advising Student support services Online engagement strategies Leadership development Community-building initiatives Academic success support Program coordination and logistics The successful candidate will also serve as a key contact person for online scholars and help improve the delivery and assessment of virtual learning initiatives. Key Responsibilities and Essential Duties The Coordinator will perform a broad range of responsibilities to ensure scholars receive effective academic and community support throughout their programs. Core Duties Include: 1. Program Planning and Coordination Plan and facilitate major scholar activities and signature programs. Coordinate orientation programs, monthly scholar meetings, and immersive summer experiences. Work collaboratively with the Program Manager and broader Scholars Program team. 2. Student Advising and Academic Support Serve as the primary contact person for online graduate scholars. Monitor scholar academic performance and engagement. Coordinate interventions and support systems with faculty, staff, and academic departments. 3. Virtual Engagement and Community Building Lead reflective discussions and virtual engagement activities. Promote critical thinking and community development among scholars. Foster meaningful online learning experiences and peer connections. 4. Program Logistics and Operations Coordinate logistics for both virtual and in-person program components. Manage communication between scholars, faculty, staff, and external partners. Support the smooth delivery of scholar services and activities. 5. Student Leadership and Social Innovation Support Increase scholar awareness and participation in: Volunteer opportunities Service initiatives Entrepreneurship programs Social innovation activities 6. Administrative and Reporting Responsibilities Maintain accurate student records and program data. Prepare reports and assist with budget-related tracking. Ensure program objectives align with timelines and funding guidelines. 7. Additional Assigned Duties Carry out other responsibilities as assigned by the program leadership. Minimum Qualifications Required Applicants must meet all the following requirements to be considered for the role: Educational Qualification Candidates must possess a Master’s degree in one of the following or related fields: Education Public Administration Social Work Instructional Design Global Development Related disciplines Mastercard Foundation Scholars Alumni Requirement Applicants must be alumni of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program and should have completed the scholarship at either: Undergraduate level, or Graduate level Additional Mandatory Requirements Applicants must: Demonstrate the ability to work independently with minimal supervision. Be currently residing within the African continent. Preferred Qualifications and Skills While not mandatory, the following qualifications and experiences will strengthen an application: Experience in online learning design and virtual facilitation Knowledge of instructional content creation Scholarship program coordination experience Familiarity with Sub-Saharan African youth initiatives Strong decision-making and stakeholder management skills Experience supporting online classrooms or digital student engagement Excellent communication and collaboration abilities Work Environment and Expectations The Coordinator role is fully remote and requires a high level of professionalism, flexibility, and collaboration. Applicants Should Expect: A dynamic virtual work environment Independent and team-based responsibilities Occasional evening or early morning work hours to accommodate global time zones Cross-functional collaboration with international teams Regular communication with scholars, staff, and institutional partners The role is ideal for professionals who thrive in mission-driven educational environments and are passionate about empowering African youth through higher education. Salary and Benefits The selected candidate will receive a competitive salary ranging between: 36,000,000 RWF and 43,000,000 RWF Beyond financial compensation, the opportunity also offers: International work exposure Leadership development experience Direct contribution to African education and youth empowerment Collaboration with global academic professionals Experience managing high-impact virtual learning initiatives Required Application Materials Interested candidates must submit the following documents: Updated Resume Cover Letter Three Professional References How to Apply Applicants should send their application materials via email to: keplerasuscholars@asu.edu Email Subject Line Africa Based Coordinator Position: First_Last Name Application Deadline The deadline to apply for this opportunity is: May 25, 2026, at 12:00 PM GMT Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid missing the deadline. Why This Opportunity Matters This position represents more than just employment. It is an opportunity to directly impact the educational journeys of African scholars while helping shape future leaders across the continent. By joining the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at ASU, the selected candidate will become part of a transformative initiative focused on: Educational equity Leadership development Youth empowerment Innovation Community impact For Mastercard Foundation Scholars alumni seeking meaningful, mission-driven careers in education and student development, this opportunity provides an ideal platform for growth and impact. Fore more information about the opportunity, Click Here Discover more job opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post ASU and Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Announce Remote Africa-Based Coordinator Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OECD Internship Programme 2026: Fully Flexible International Internship Opportunity for Students Worldwide
summary: The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Internship Programme, offering students from around the world an opportunity to work alongside global policy experts and specialists in an international environment. This internship programme provides students with hands-on experience in policy development, research, data analysis, communication, administration, and international cooperation while contributing to the OECD’s mission of building better policies for better lives. The OECD Internship Programme is designed for motivated and talented students who are currently enrolled in bachelor’s, master’s, or PhD programmes and wish to strengthen their analytical, technical, and professional skills within one of the world’s most influential international organisations. Unlike many internship opportunities with strict deadlines, the OECD Internship Programme accepts applications on a rolling basis throughout the year, making it easier for eligible candidates to apply at a convenient time. Applicants can work in-person in Paris, remotely, or in hybrid arrangements depending on the needs of the hiring team, making this one of the most flexible international internship opportunities currently available. About the OECD Internship Programme The OECD Internship Programme aims to bring diverse and curious students into the organisation to support its work across multiple policy sectors and corporate functions. Interns become part of a multicultural and international environment where they collaborate with economists, policy analysts, communication experts, researchers, and government specialists. The organisation works closely with governments worldwide on major global issues including: Artificial intelligence Climate change Education and skills Economic development International trade Taxation Digital transformation Science and innovation Employment Governance Environment Health systems Regional development Finance and investment Interns contribute directly to projects that influence policies affecting millions of people globally. What Interns Will Do Selected interns may be assigned responsibilities such as: Research and Analysis Conducting research on policy issues Reviewing existing materials and reports Collecting, organising, and analysing data Supporting evidence-based policy recommendations Documentation and Publications Preparing studies and policy documents Drafting notes and technical annexes Assisting with publications and working papers Supporting report development processes Meetings and Networking Participating in international meetings and seminars Liaising with government officials and researchers Building networks with international organisations Collaborating with member country delegations Event and Communication Support Assisting with event planning and coordination Supporting communication campaigns Helping with planning and evaluation activities Areas Where Interns May Work Policy Areas Interns may support policy analysts in departments such as: Agriculture and Fisheries Climate Change Development Digital Economy Education and Skills Employment Environment Finance and Investment Governance Health Industry, Business and Entrepreneurship Science, Technology and Innovation Society Taxation Trade Regional, Rural and Urban Development International Energy Agency Nuclear Energy Agency International Transport Forum Corporate Functions Students with administrative or operational backgrounds may also work in: Audit Communications and Public Relations Event Management Finance and Accounting Human Resources Information Technology Legal Services Office Administration Translation Services Internship Duration The OECD Internship Programme runs for: Between 1 and 6 months initially Renewable up to 12 months total Internships are generally: Full-time (40 hours per week) Occasionally part-time (minimum 20 hours weekly under exceptional circumstances) The OECD strongly encourages students to pursue internships lasting at least four months to maximize the experience. Internship Location and Work Flexibility Most internships are based at the OECD headquarters in Paris, France. However, the programme offers flexible arrangements including: In-person internships Hybrid internships Fully remote internships The working arrangement depends on the hiring department’s needs. Financial Benefits Successful interns located in Paris receive financial support including: Monthly Allowance €1,000 per full month worked Leave Benefits 2.5 working days of leave per full month worked French public holidays observed These benefits help interns manage living expenses while gaining international work experience. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the OECD Internship Programme, applicants must: Academic Requirements Be enrolled full-time in an accredited degree programme Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD students are eligible Remain enrolled throughout the internship duration Skills and Competencies Applicants should possess: Strong analytical and research skills Good quantitative and IT skills Excellent drafting and communication abilities Ability to work in multicultural teams Language Requirements Candidates must have: Excellent command of either English or French Working knowledge of the second official language or willingness to learn it Knowledge of additional languages is considered an advantage. Important Note on Accredited Programmes The OECD does not recognise: Non-degree online courses Programmes without physical attendance requirements Non-accredited academic programmes Applicants must therefore ensure their institution and programme meet accreditation standards. Selection Process The internship recruitment process involves four stages: 1. Online Application Candidates submit: Online application form Cover letter via the SmartRecruiters platform 2. Pre-Screening Applications are reviewed by the Internship Office and shortlisted for directorates. 3. Interview or Written Test Hiring teams may conduct: Interviews Written assessments 4. Internship Offer Selected candidates receive: Internship agreement Official offer documentation Rolling Applications and Recruitment Timeline One major advantage of the OECD Internship Programme is its rolling application system. Applications are accepted year-round, allowing students flexibility in planning their internship period. However, candidates should apply well in advance because: The recruitment process may take up to three months Hiring depends on departmental needs and candidate availability Applicants can also update their availability after submission through their application portal. Why You Should Apply for the OECD Internship Programme The OECD Internship Programme offers numerous benefits including: International Exposure Work within a globally recognised international organisation. Professional Networking Connect with policymakers, researchers, economists, and international experts. Career Development Build technical, analytical, and communication skills highly valued worldwide. Flexible Work Options Choose between remote, hybrid, or in-person work arrangements depending on availability. Real Policy Impact Contribute to policy solutions addressing global challenges. APPLY HERE Deadline There is no fixed deadline . Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year. Final Thoughts The OECD Internship Programme remains one of the most prestigious international internship opportunities for students seeking global exposure, policy experience, and professional growth. Whether your interests lie in economics, climate change, governance, technology, communications, finance, or international development, the programme provides a unique platform to gain practical experience while contributing to meaningful global initiatives. Students from diverse academic and cultural backgrounds are encouraged to apply and become part of an organisation committed to shaping better policies for better lives. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internships Opportunities on OFY here . The post OECD Internship Programme 2026: Fully Flexible International Internship Opportunity for Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: YES-MED Call for Participants 2026: International Youth Empowerment Programme for Mediterranean Youth in Social and Solidarity Economy
summary: Young people across the Mediterranean region now have the opportunity to participate in the transformative YES-MED Project 2026, an international initiative focused on youth empowerment, employability, entrepreneurship, social innovation, and sustainable development. The project, officially titled Youth Employment in Solidarity – Mixing Experiences and Diversity (YES-MED) , aims to strengthen the socio-economic integration of young people by equipping them with practical skills, international exposure, and opportunities within the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) sector. The YES-MED project is designed for young individuals aged 18 to 35 from six Mediterranean countries and offers an engaging learning journey that combines training, networking, entrepreneurship, international cooperation, sustainability, and youth-led action. With a focus on creating socially responsible employment opportunities and fostering sustainable community development, YES-MED will engage 120 young participants in a dynamic training and mobility programme between September and October 2026. What is the YES-MED Project? The YES-MED Project is an international youth empowerment initiative implemented by seven partner organizations across the Mediterranean region. The project seeks to create stronger pathways for youth employment and active participation in society through Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) models and international cooperation. The initiative recognizes the growing need for sustainable employment opportunities for youth while also addressing social inclusion, environmental responsibility, entrepreneurship, and community resilience. Through a comprehensive training pathway and mobility opportunities, YES-MED aims to empower young people to become active changemakers capable of designing and implementing impactful initiatives in their local communities and beyond. Participating Countries The YES-MED project is open to young people from the following Mediterranean countries: Italy France Spain Tunisia Morocco Lebanon Participants from these countries will collaborate in an intercultural and international learning environment that promotes diversity, inclusion, cooperation, and shared experiences. Main Objective of the YES-MED Programme The overall goal of the YES-MED initiative is to strengthen the socio-economic integration of young people aged 18–35 by: Enhancing employability skills Promoting entrepreneurship Encouraging sustainable and eco-conscious practices Supporting youth participation in Social and Solidarity Economy initiatives Building international cooperation networks Empowering youth to lead community-based projects Encouraging socially responsible innovation The programme seeks to create long-term positive impact by enabling participants to transfer their knowledge and skills into practical actions within their communities. The Youth Mediterranean Empowerment Path (WP3) One of the core components of the YES-MED project is the Youth Mediterranean Empowerment Path , an international learning journey specifically designed for young multipliers. A total of 120 young participants will be selected to join this intensive training programme. Duration of the Training Participants will complete approximately: 40 hours of training Between September and October 2026 The programme is jointly designed by project partners and tailored to participants’ interests, backgrounds, and local realities. What Participants Will Learn The YES-MED training programme combines theoretical knowledge with practical learning approaches. Participants will gain valuable competencies in areas that are increasingly important for sustainable development and future employment. Key Training Areas Include: 1. Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) Participants will explore alternative economic models focused on: Social impact Community development Inclusion Sustainability Ethical entrepreneurship They will learn how SSE initiatives can help address social and economic challenges within Mediterranean communities. 2. International Cooperation The programme introduces participants to the principles and practices of international collaboration, including: Partnership building Cross-cultural cooperation Community engagement International development perspectives 3. Digital Skills Development Participants will improve essential digital competencies that can support: Entrepreneurship Communication Project management Online collaboration Digital innovation 4. Environmental Sustainability and Eco-Conscious Practices The YES-MED project places strong emphasis on environmental responsibility. Participants will learn about: Sustainable development Eco-conscious entrepreneurship Green practices Environmental awareness Sustainable community initiatives 5. Project Cycle Management Participants will gain practical understanding of: Project planning Project implementation Monitoring and evaluation Reporting Risk management These skills are essential for successfully leading social initiatives and community projects. 6. Business Model Development Young participants will learn how to: Develop sustainable business ideas Design social enterprises Build innovative community solutions Understand entrepreneurial strategies 7. Financial Literacy The programme also strengthens financial management skills, including: Budgeting Financial planning Resource management Basic financial sustainability principles Pilot Actions and Community Impact Following the training programme, participants will move into the implementation phase known as WP4. During this phase, participants will apply the knowledge and competencies gained throughout the programme by designing and implementing pilot actions. These initiatives may be: Local National International The pilot projects will be youth-led and community-focused, enabling participants to become active contributors to positive social change. This hands-on approach aims to create: Sustainable impact Community engagement Youth leadership Social innovation Multiplier effects across communities Transnational Youth Mobility Scheme (WP5) Another exciting component of the YES-MED project is the Transnational Youth Mobility Scheme . From the 120 participants, 24 young people will be selected to participate in this international mobility experience. Objectives of the Mobility Scheme The programme aims to: Strengthen cooperation between youth workers Connect participants with SSE enterprises Foster relationships with civil society organizations Encourage intercultural dialogue Build long-term professional networks Benefits of the Mobility Programme Participants selected for WP5 will benefit from: International exposure Networking opportunities Exchange of experiences and perspectives Professional development Collaboration with international actors Immersive learning experiences The mobility programme is expected to contribute to the creation of a robust Mediterranean youth network focused on sustainability, social entrepreneurship, and cooperation. Why Young People Should Apply The YES-MED programme offers much more than a traditional training experience. It provides young people with opportunities to: Develop practical and future-oriented skills Increase employability Gain international experience Build professional networks Strengthen leadership capacities Become active changemakers in their communities Participate in sustainable and socially responsible initiatives Learn from international experts and peers For youth interested in entrepreneurship, sustainability, social innovation, international cooperation, and community development, YES-MED represents a valuable platform for growth and impact. Application Deadline Deadline for Submission: 31 May 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early before the deadline. Final Thoughts The YES-MED Call for Participants 2026 is an outstanding opportunity for young people across the Mediterranean region to strengthen their professional capacities, expand their international networks, and contribute to sustainable social transformation. By combining training, entrepreneurship, environmental sustainability, international cooperation, and youth leadership, the programme equips participants with tools that can create meaningful impact in their communities and across the Mediterranean region. Young leaders, aspiring entrepreneurs, youth workers, and changemakers passionate about sustainability and social innovation are highly encouraged to apply and become part of this international learning journey. Application Link Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post YES-MED Call for Participants 2026: International Youth Empowerment Programme for Mediterranean Youth in Social and Solidarity Economy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded King’s College London PhD Studentship 2026 on Justice and Energy Transitions: Study Critical Minerals Governance with Annual Stipend and Research Support
summary: The King s College London is now accepting applications for a fully funded PhD studentship under the African Leadership Centre focused on justice, governance, and energy transitions in the context of critical minerals extraction. This prestigious doctoral opportunity is part of the UKRI Future Leaders Fellowship project titled Justice in Critical Minerals Governance and Energy Transitions , led by Dr Clement Sefa Nyarko. The PhD studentship offers a unique opportunity for researchers passionate about environmental justice, governance, sustainability, natural resource politics, and community-centered research to contribute to global discussions surrounding clean energy transitions and critical mineral extraction. The successful candidate will begin studies in October 2026 and will receive full financial support, including tuition fees, stipend, and research funding. About the King’s College London PhD Studentship As the world accelerates toward net-zero emissions and clean energy technologies, the demand for critical minerals such as lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements continues to rise. However, the extraction of these minerals often comes with serious environmental, political, and social consequences, especially for communities located in resource-rich regions. This PhD project seeks to critically examine these challenges through interpretive and community-centered research approaches. The selected doctoral student will investigate how issues of justice, equity, participation, governance, and accountability intersect with global energy transitions. The studentship is hosted by the African Leadership Centre (ALC) within the School of Global Affairs at King’s College London. The ALC is internationally recognized for promoting ethical, African-led, and innovative approaches to leadership, development, peace, and security. Students joining this program will become part of a vibrant academic and policy-oriented community while receiving extensive doctoral training and mentorship. Funding Benefits The selected PhD candidate will receive a comprehensive funding package that includes: Full Home tuition fees coverage Annual stipend of £23,805 paid at the UKRI rate Research support allowance of £1,350 per year Access to specialist doctoral training Academic mentorship from leading scholars Support for conferences, fieldwork, and publications Opportunities to collaborate with international research and policy partners Potential field engagement opportunities in Africa, Latin America, or Australia depending on research design This funding package allows the student to focus fully on research and academic development throughout the duration of the program. Duration and Start Date Start Date: October 2026 Duration: 3.5 years (Full-time) Research Themes and Areas of Focus Applicants are encouraged to align their research interests with the broader goals of the fellowship while developing their own independent doctoral research agenda. Potential research themes include: 1. Justice and Equity in Critical Mineral Supply Chains Research may explore how benefits and burdens associated with mineral extraction are distributed across societies and communities. 2. Community Experiences of Justice Applicants can investigate how local populations interpret justice, exclusion, and participation in extractive industries. 3. Governance and Accountability in Extractive Contexts This theme focuses on political leadership, transparency, governance structures, and institutional accountability surrounding natural resource extraction. 4. Participatory and Bottom-Up Energy Transition Approaches Researchers may examine inclusive and community-led approaches to studying sustainable energy transitions. 5. Innovative Interpretive and Qualitative Methodologies The fellowship welcomes innovative research approaches including: Hermeneutical Ethnography Interpretive methodologies Narrative analysis Symbolic analysis Community-centered qualitative research Why This PhD Opportunity Matters The global shift toward renewable energy is increasing international demand for minerals essential for batteries, electric vehicles, and clean technologies. While these transitions are often presented as environmentally beneficial, communities living in mining regions frequently face: Environmental degradation Land displacement Exclusion from policy decisions Unequal distribution of economic benefits Political marginalization Social injustices This PhD aims to contribute to more ethical and equitable approaches to global energy transitions by centering community voices and lived experiences. Academic Environment at the African Leadership Centre The African Leadership Centre at King’s College London offers a dynamic environment for doctoral researchers interested in global development, governance, leadership, and justice. Students will benefit from: Interdisciplinary supervision Leadership development opportunities Networking with global scholars Access to international policy communities Advanced methodological training Collaborative academic culture The Centre is particularly known for supporting African-led perspectives and ethical scholarship. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet several academic and research-related requirements to be considered for the studentship. Essential Requirements Candidates should possess: A Master’s degree (completed or near completion) in: International Development Political Science Environmental Studies Geography Sociology Anthropology Law Or related disciplines Applicants must also demonstrate: Strong interest in interpretive, ethnographic, or community-centered research approaches Understanding of narratives, symbols, silences, and non-verbal meaning-making processes Ability or potential to use participatory and co-creation methodologies Experience using qualitative or multimodal analysis software such as: NVivo Atlas.ti MAXQDA Interest in: Environmental justice Energy transitions Extractive politics Natural resource governance Desirable Qualifications The following experiences are considered advantageous: Excellent communication skills Previous fieldwork experience in Africa, Latin America, Australia, or other extractive regions Background in sustainability transitions Indigenous studies expertise Environmental governance knowledge Application Process The application process consists of two separate stages. Part 1: Submit Documents by Email Applicants must send a single PDF file via email to: clement.1.sefa-nyarko@kcl.ac.uk Subject Line: Application for PhD Studentship on Justice in Energy Transitions The PDF should include: Research Proposal (Maximum 2,000 words) Research focus Questions Case studies Significance Methodological innovation Bibliography (excluded from word count) Sample of Written Work Personal Statement (1–2 pages) Academic Transcripts Two References At least one academic reference Professional references accepted if qualifications were completed more than five years ago Part 2: Submit Formal King’s Apply Application Applicants must also submit an official application through King’s Apply to the PhD programme: Leadership Studies with Reference to Security and Development at the African Leadership Centre. Deadline for Applications Application Deadline: 25 May 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their materials early. Who Should Apply? This PhD studentship is ideal for: Early-career researchers Graduates passionate about environmental justice Scholars interested in energy governance Researchers focusing on extractive politics Students committed to participatory and community-centered methodologies Individuals seeking to influence equitable global sustainability transitions Final Thoughts The King’s College London PhD Studentship on Justice and Energy Transitions represents a powerful opportunity for aspiring scholars to contribute to urgent global conversations surrounding critical minerals, climate justice, and equitable sustainability transitions. With generous funding, international research exposure, advanced doctoral training, and mentorship from leading academics, this studentship offers an exceptional platform for researchers committed to advancing justice-centered approaches to global energy transitions. Students with strong interdisciplinary interests and passion for ethical, community-driven research are highly encouraged to apply before the deadline. Application Link Part 1: Email a single PDF to clement.1.sefa-nyarko@kcl.ac.uk by 25 May 2026 with the subject line: Application for PhD Studentship on Justice in Energy Transitions . Include: Research proposal (max. 2,000 words) outlining focus, questions, cases, significance, and methodological innovation, plus bibliography (not included in word count). Sample of written work. Personal statement (1–2 pages). Academic transcript(s). • Two references (at least one academic; professional references acceptable if qualifications were completed over five years ago). Part 2: Submit an application via King’s Apply to the PhD programme Leadership Studies with Reference to Security and Development at the African Leadership Centre. Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global PhD opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Fully Funded King’s College London PhD Studentship 2026 on Justice and Energy Transitions: Study Critical Minerals Governance with Annual Stipend and Research Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants 2026: Up to $20,000 for Political Science PhD Research in the United States
summary: The American Political Science Association (APSA) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants (DDRIG), a prestigious funding opportunity designed to support doctoral candidates conducting advanced research in political science. The program is funded by the National Science Foundation (NSF) and aims to strengthen groundbreaking doctoral research that improves understanding of citizenship, governance, politics, and public policy. The APSA DDRIG program has become one of the most competitive and respected dissertation funding opportunities available to political science PhD students in the United States. Since 2020, the program has already supported 139 doctoral candidates from 53 PhD-granting institutions across the country. For the 2026 cycle, APSA intends to award between 20 and 25 grants ranging from $10,000 to $20,000 each. The application cycle officially opened on April 1, 2026, and the deadline for submission is June 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Time. What is the APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grant? The APSA DDRIG program is designed to enhance and improve doctoral dissertation research in political science. The grant specifically supports empirically oriented and theoretically grounded research projects that contribute meaningful knowledge to the field of politics and governance. The funding supports research that explores areas such as: Citizenship and democracy Government institutions Political behavior Public policy International relations Political economy Race, ethnicity, and politics Gender and politics Comparative politics Political violence and conflict Urban and local politics Indigenous politics Public administration Political institutions APSA also welcomes emerging methodologies and innovative approaches, including projects involving artificial intelligence tools and large language models in political science research. Funding Benefits Successful applicants will receive substantial financial and professional support through the program. The grant offers several benefits including: 1. Research Funding Awardees will receive grants ranging between $10,000 and $20,000 to support dissertation-related research expenses. 2. Professional Development Opportunities Beyond direct financial support, APSA connects recipients to: Professional development programs Public engagement networks Academic mentorship opportunities Research visibility platforms Networking opportunities within the political science community 3. Career Advancement Receiving an APSA DDRIG award significantly strengthens an academic CV and demonstrates research excellence, which can help scholars secure: Future grants Postdoctoral opportunities Academic appointments Research collaborations 4. National Recognition The award is highly competitive and nationally recognized within the field of political science, giving recipients additional credibility in academia and research circles. Who Can Apply? The APSA DDRIG program is open to graduate students who meet the eligibility requirements established by APSA and NSF. Applicants must: Be enrolled in a doctoral degree-granting university. Be studying at a university accredited in the United States and physically located in the United States. Have completed all preliminary doctoral requirements, including: Comprehensive exams Dissertation prospectus defense Be conducting empirical dissertation research related to citizenship, government, or politics. Be sponsored by their dissertation adviser. Importantly, international students are also eligible to Apply , provided they are enrolled at a qualifying U.S.-based university. What Research Areas Are Supported? The APSA DDRIG program supports a wide range of political science research methodologies and themes. APSA encourages diversity in research topics, institutions, and methodologies. Supported methodologies include: Quantitative research Qualitative research Experimental studies Historical and archival research Ethnographic research Interview-based fieldwork AI-assisted political science research The program focuses on basic research rather than applied research. According to APSA, funded projects should primarily aim to generate foundational knowledge and understanding rather than immediate practical applications. Required Application Materials Applicants are required to submit a comprehensive proposal package. Required materials include: Proposal summary Research plan and project description Budget and budget justification Professional development plan Data management plan Adviser support form Institutional support form Research ethics documentation Information about other funding sources Applicants are advised to carefully review formatting and submission guidelines before applying. APSA’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion APSA has emphasized its commitment to expanding participation and diversity within political science research. The organization aims to support promising doctoral candidates from: Diverse geographic regions Different institutional backgrounds Underrepresented communities Various methodological traditions Emerging research areas This commitment aligns with APSA’s broader strategic mission to strengthen inclusive scholarship in political science. Important Deadline Application Deadline: June 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Time. Late applications are unlikely to be accepted, so applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early. How to Apply Interested candidates can access the application portal and detailed eligibility requirements through the official APSA website . Eligibility Information: Eligibility Requirements Application Guidelines: Application Materials and Format FAQs: APSA DDRIG FAQs The APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants represent an outstanding opportunity for political science doctoral candidates seeking funding, visibility, and academic advancement. With generous research support, national recognition, and professional development opportunities, the program continues to shape the next generation of political science scholars in the United States. Eligible Applicants PhD students in Political Science and related fields Doctoral researchers conducting dissertation research U.S.-based graduate students International students studying at accredited U.S. universities Eligible Countries/Regions United States-based universities Open to applicants of all nationalities studying in the U.S. Required Qualifications Enrollment in a doctoral degree program University must be accredited and physically located in the United States Completion of comprehensive exams Approved dissertation prospectus Empirical dissertation research focus Dissertation adviser sponsorship Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY here The post APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants 2026: Up to $20,000 for Political Science PhD Research in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OPCW Science Internship 2026 in The Netherlands: International Internship Opportunity for Science Students and Graduates
summary: The Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Science Internship within the Office of Strategy and Policy (OSP) for 2026. This prestigious international internship program offers science students and recent graduates an opportunity to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organisations dedicated to chemical disarmament, global security, and peaceful scientific advancement. The internship is based in The Hague, Netherlands, and is aimed at motivated young scientists who are interested in international affairs, science diplomacy, emerging technologies, policy development, and the implementation of the Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC). According to the official announcement, three science internship positions are available, with start dates beginning in mid-July 2026. This internship represents an excellent opportunity for students and graduates who wish to build careers in science policy, international organisations, diplomacy, chemistry, technology governance, and global security. About the OPCW Science Internship Programme The OPCW internship programme is designed to provide participants with first-hand experience in the daily operations of an international organisation. Interns work directly with experienced professionals and experts while contributing to important scientific and policy-related initiatives. The Office of Strategy and Policy (OSP), where the internship is hosted, focuses on major cross-cutting issues affecting the implementation of the Chemical Weapons Convention. One of its key responsibilities is monitoring scientific and technological developments that could impact chemical disarmament, international security, and global policy frameworks. As part of the internship, selected candidates will gain exposure to: International scientific policy discussions Science diplomacy initiatives Emerging technologies Global chemical security Policy development processes Research and analytical work International conferences and meetings Interns will also collaborate with professionals across different OPCW departments and participate in organisational events and discussions whenever opportunities arise. Key Responsibilities of the Internship The scientific work carried out within the Office of Strategy and Policy is broad and dynamic. Selected interns will contribute to several important tasks and projects. Responsibilities may include: 1. Supporting Science for Diplomats Events Interns will help develop ideas and activities for “Science for Diplomats” events. These initiatives aim to communicate complex scientific and technological concepts to diplomats and policymakers in an understandable way. 2.
