Monitoring Scientific and Technological Developments Participants will research and monitor emerging scientific and technological trends relevant to the OPCW’s work and the Chemical Weapons Convention. 3. Assisting the Scientific Advisory Board Interns may support the work associated with the OPCW Scientific Advisory Board and its Temporary Working Groups. 4. Preparing Reports and Presentations The role includes assisting with the preparation of: Reports Briefing notes Presentations Internal policy documents Outreach materials 5. Graphic Design and Communication Support Interns may also assist in designing graphics, layouts, and visual communication materials for outreach and documentation purposes. Internship Learning Benefits The OPCW states that the internship is tailored according to the intern’s academic background and skillset. The programme focuses on three major areas of development: Skill Development Interns will strengthen practical skills such as: Communication Research Scientific analysis Problem-solving Graphic design Policy writing Career Development Participants will gain a deeper understanding of: International organisations Science diplomacy Global governance Chemical weapons disarmament International policymaking Personal Development The internship also helps participants improve: Self-confidence Teamwork Networking Time management Professional adaptability These experiences can significantly strengthen a candidate’s future career prospects in science, diplomacy, international relations, public policy, and research. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be enrolled in a university degree programme in a scientific discipline and have completed at least one year of study; or Be enrolled in a graduate school programme (second university degree or higher); or Have graduated from a scientific discipline within one year before the internship begins. Additionally, candidates must: Be citizens of an OPCW Member State Have strong interest in science and technology Possess fluency in English Applications from candidates with non-scientific backgrounds will not be considered. Although a background in chemical sciences is desirable, it is not mandatory. The organisation also values interest in emerging technologies and their applications within chemical contexts. Language Requirements English fluency is essential for the internship. However, knowledge of any additional OPCW official languages is considered an advantage. Desirable languages include: Arabic Chinese French Russian Spanish Important Internship Conditions Applicants should carefully note that the OPCW internship programme is unpaid. According to the organisation: Interns do not receive financial remuneration Travel expenses are not covered Accommodation and living costs are the intern’s responsibility Visa and relocation arrangements must be handled by the intern Interns must prove they have sufficient financial resources during the internship period. The internship follows normal OPCW working schedules, with interns working up to 40 hours per week. Why This Internship Matters The OPCW is globally recognised for its efforts toward eliminating chemical weapons and promoting peaceful uses of chemistry. In 2013, the organisation received the Nobel Peace Prize for its extensive work in chemical disarmament. Participating in an OPCW internship provides young scientists with rare exposure to: International diplomacy Science-policy collaboration Global security initiatives High-level international networking Real-world policy applications of science For students and graduates seeking careers in global organisations such as the United Nations, scientific institutions, policy think tanks, or international NGOs, this internship can serve as a major stepping stone. Application Deadline The official vacancy notice states that applications must be submitted before the closing date indicated on the OPCW CandidateSpace portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early because submissions close at 22:00 Netherlands local time on the final day. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply directly through the official OPCW careers portal . Click here to find more information about this internship opportunity on the OPCW website Applicants are required to: Select the vacancy Complete the OPCW Candidate Application Template Upload required documents Complete the online application form. Eligible Applicants Undergraduate science students Graduate students Recent science graduates Young researchers Early-career scientists Eligible Countries/Regions Citizens of OPCW Member States worldwide Applicants from Africa, Europe, Asia, North America, South America, and Oceania can apply if their country is an OPCW Member State Required Qualifications Scientific academic background Completion of at least one year of university study Strong English language skills Interest in science policy and international affairs Interest in emerging technologies and chemical sciences Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY here The post OPCW Science Internship 2026 in The Netherlands: International Internship Opportunity for Science Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
